about.bio_html:
Security researcher with 10+ years in the field spanning both red and blue teams. I specialize in malware analysis, reverse engineering, client-side threat detection, and WAF engineering.

I've worked in SOCs (Tier 1–3), built IPS/IDS signatures, and currently focus on web application firewall engineering and cloud-scale threat intelligence at Cloudflare. I compete with Water Paddlers in CTFs, finishing 2nd at DEF CON three consecutive years (2023–2025).

experience.desc: WAF rule engineering, zero-day response, client-side threat research, and AI-powered detection.
Malware analysis, SOC operations, IPS/IDS rule development, exploit analysis, and threat intelligence.

advisories.title: HtmlSanitizer URL Attributes Pass Through BiDi Override Characters → Visual href Spoofing
advisories.desc: UrlSanitizer::parse() accepted URLs containing Unicode explicit-direction BiDi formatting characters (U+202A–U+202E, U+2066–U+2069) and passed them unchanged into href/src attributes. When rendered, the override characters reverse the visual ordering of the URL text — the displayed link can differ arbitrarily from the actual destination. Classic visual-spoofing / phishing primitive against viewers of sanitized content.

advisories.title: Mailjet and LOX24 Webhook Parsers Never Verify the Configured Secret → Unauthenticated Webhook Event Injection
advisories.desc: Both bridges' doParse() methods received the configured webhook secret but never read it; the payload was decoded and returned unconditionally. Any POST to the webhook endpoint was accepted even when a secret was configured, allowing forged bounce / spam / open / click / delivery events — suppression-list corruption, delivery-metrics fraud, etc.

advisories.title: Mailtrap Webhook Parser Never Verifies the X-Mt-Signature HMAC → Unauthenticated Webhook Event Injection
advisories.desc: The Mailtrap mailer bridge's webhook parser received the configured webhook secret but never read it, ignoring the X-Mt-Signature HMAC header Mailtrap sends with every request. Any POST to the webhook endpoint was accepted, enabling unauthenticated injection of forged delivery / bounce / open / click / spam events.

advisories.title: JsonPath Evaluates Attacker-Controlled Regular Expressions in match()/search() Without Limits → ReDoS
advisories.desc: JsonPath's match() and search() filter functions compiled caller-supplied patterns directly into preg_match() with no length cap, no RFC 9485 i-regexp restriction, and no backtrack bound. A pathological pattern such as $[?search(@, "(a+)+$")] pinned a CPU core for seconds per request — a handful of concurrent requests exhausted the worker pool, yielding denial-of-service. Suppressed PCRE errors meant the attack left no log trace.

advisories.title: Twilio Webhook Parser Never Verifies the X-Twilio-Signature HMAC → Unauthenticated Webhook Event Injection
advisories.desc: The Twilio SMS notifier bridge's webhook parser received the configured webhook secret but never read it, ignoring the X-Twilio-Signature HMAC header Twilio sends with every request. Any POST to the webhook endpoint was accepted, enabling unauthenticated injection of forged delivered / failed / undelivered status events with downstream impact on metrics and automation triggers.

advisories.title: Symlink Confinement Bypass in resources.Get → Arbitrary File Read
advisories.desc: A regression introduced in v0.123.0 caused RootMappingFs.statRoot to call Stat (which follows symlinks) instead of Lstat, so a direct resources.Get "somefile" where somefile was a symlink pointing outside the mount would return the target's contents. A symlink planted inside a theme or local mount (e.g. a locally-vendored theme under themes/) could therefore read arbitrary files reachable to the user running hugo. Themes mounted as Go modules from GitHub have symlinks stripped and are not affected. Fixed in v0.162.0, which calls LstatIfPossible and rejects symlinked entries.

advisories.title: security.http.urls Allow-List Bypass via HTTP Redirects → SSRF
advisories.desc: resources.GetRemote enforced security.http.urls on the URL it was called with, but until v0.162.0 it did not re-validate intermediate URLs on HTTP 3xx redirects. An allowed server (or an attacker controlling its DNS or response) could redirect the request to a host the policy was meant to forbid — for example http://localhost/ or an internal IP — and Hugo would fetch from the redirected target, lifting any host-shape restriction the operator had set. Affected v0.91.0 through v0.161.1. Fixed in v0.162.0, which installs a CheckRedirect that re-runs the policy on every redirect target and caps the chain at 10 hops.

advisories.title: Default CAPTCHA Missing Invalidation → Challenge Replay / CAPTCHA Bypass
advisories.desc: The inbuilt MyBB Default CAPTCHA did not consistently enforce single-use semantics. Several endpoints — Contact Us (contact.php), Resend Account Activation, Reset Password and Email User (member.php), and Send to Friend (sendthread.php) — never called captcha::invalidate_captcha() after a successful validation, allowing remote attackers to reuse the same valid CAPTCHA response across multiple requests and endpoints until it was invalidated elsewhere or expired. CWE-837 (Improper Enforcement of a Single, Unique Action). Fixed in MyBB 1.8.40.
